title: INVERSE SYSTEMS
summary: This page explains how the commutative property of LTIC systems implies that integrating or differentiating the input causes the output to be integrated or differentiated correspondingly. It then introduces inverse systems, derives h(t) ∗ hi(t) = δ(t), defines the unit step response g(t) as the integral of h(t), and shows that y(t) can be computed as ẋ(t) ∗ g(t).
